plan: 1. In `src/main.ts`: Remove the creation of `wireframe` (createWireframePolyhedra) and `cubeLattice` (createCubeLatticeWireframe) system instances (lines ~131-153). Remove their SingleRotationEntry items from `singleEntries` array (lines 174, 176). Remove their entries from `systemRegistry` (lines 188, 190). Remove the corresponding imports at the top of the file.

2. In `src/visual/compoundModes.ts`: Remove the compound mode definitions that reference the retired systems — `'cloud+wireframe'` (uses wirepolyhedra, lines 29-35) and `'pointcloud+cubelattice'` (uses cubelattice, lines 43-49). Remove the `wirepolyhedra` and `cubelattice` cases from `primaryCountForSystem` switch (lines 72-73).

3. Add a comment at the top of `src/visual/systems/wireframePolyhedra.ts` and `src/visual/systems/cubeLatticeWireframe.ts` explaining they are retired from rotation (rejected twice by user, US-069/US-071 and US-068/US-072 respectively) and left unregistered intentionally. Do NOT delete the files — they may serve as reference.
4. Similarly add a brief retirement comment at the top of `src/visual/generators/cubeLattice.ts` noting the generator is unused since the cube lattice mode was retired.

5. Verify remaining rotation composition. After removal, the rotation will have: **Single modes (7)**: particles, ribbon, pointcloud, crystal, flowribbon, fractalgrowth, terrain. **Compound modes (2, medium/high only)**: particles+flowribbon, pointcloud+fractalgrowth. This gives 7 modes on low tier and 9 on medium/high — well above the 3-mode minimum.

6. In `src/visual/quality.ts`: Verify that quality profile properties only used by the retired modes (`maxPolyhedra`, `latticeGridSize`, `latticeCellSize`, `latticeVoidDensity`, `latticeJitter`, `enableElectricArc`, `arcSubdivisions`) are still referenced elsewhere (e.g., `enableElectricArc`/`arcSubdivisions` are used by constellation lines overlay). Remove only truly dead quality properties. Keep any that are still used by overlays or other systems.
7. Clean up any dead imports across touched files — grep for imports of `createWireframePolyhedra`, `createCubeLatticeWireframe`, and related types that are no longer used anywhere.
8. Run `npm run build` to confirm no compile errors and no dead imports.

9. Manual smoke test: load in browser, confirm no console errors, confirm at least 3 distinct modes appear in rotation, confirm terrain/pointcloud/other strong modes rotate correctly, confirm mute toggle and info overlay still work.
testing_recommendations: Run `npm run build` — must pass with zero errors and no unused-import warnings.
Open the app in browser and watch rotation through at least 3-4 mode switches to confirm variety and smooth transitions.
Check browser console for any errors, especially WebGL or import-related errors.
Verify no references to 'wirepolyhedra' or 'cubelattice' appear in the active rotation by adding a temporary console.log in modeManager's switch logic.
Confirm compound modes still work on medium/high quality tiers (particles+flowribbon and pointcloud+fractalgrowth).
Confirm overlay (bezier/constellation) still attaches and renders correctly since it previously could attach to wirepolyhedra-based modes.
Test on low-quality tier to ensure the 7 single modes rotate without issues.
risks: Quality profile properties like `enableElectricArc` and `arcSubdivisions` are shared between the retired wireframePolyhedra system and the constellation lines overlay — must not remove these properties from quality.ts.

Removing 2 single modes + 2 compound modes changes the rotation array length, which shifts the seeded initial mode index — existing seeds will land on different starting modes. This is acceptable but worth noting.
Generator files (polyhedraEdges.ts, dualPolyhedra.ts, nestedSolids.ts, geodesicSphere.ts) may still be imported by other systems or utilities — verify before removing any generators.
The `extractSystemConfig` function in quality.ts may have cases for the retired systems that should be cleaned up, but only if no other code path calls them.
